items.acceptanceCriteria: Equipment document is available
Daily pre-operational inspection checklists are checked (if applicable)
Preventive Maintenance is established and updated records are maintained
Are previous inspection reports checked
General fire protection is in evidence (non-combustible materials, adequate ventilation of machinery spaces)
Width of escalator is not less than 560 mm nor more 1020 mm
Geometry is checked/verified against design requirements
The angle of inclination shall be designed not to exceed 30 deg from the horizontal, but due to field conditions at the site shall be permitted to exceed this maximum by 1 deg
Handrails are 100mm (min ) horizontally and 25mm (min ) vertically away from adjacent surfaces
Entrance and egress ends are in compliance with regard to combplates , floor surfaces , safety zones and steps
Lighting intensity is not less than 50 lux (demarkation lights at both landings and lighting at exposed step treads)
Caution signs are in place at both landings
Combplates are in order
Deck barricades are in compliance with dimensional requirements
Minimum of two and maximum of 4 steps at each entrance and exit designed to carry a load of 135Kg on a 6 x 10" plate (When step is forced upward more than 5 mm , escalator shall stop)
Operating devices such as starting and inspection control switches and emergency stop switches are provided
Skirt obstruction devices are operable and cause the escalator to stop when the skirt is obstructed
Handrail speed monitoring device activates an alarm and stops the escalator when the speed difference is 15% or more for between 2 and 6 seconds
Egress restrictors (if fitted) cause the electric power to be removed from the machine when the exit restrictors begin to close
Rated speed is not to exceed 0.5m/sec (speed not to vary) (If an AC squirrel cage induction motor to drive is used a speed governor is not required)
Balustrades are installed on both sides of the escalator
Ceiling intersection guards are fitted where required and comply with various dimensional criteria
Skirt/step clearance is at least 1" (unloaded ) and a minimum of 0.2" (loaded) (For escalators without dynamic skirt panels, verify the skirt/step performance index value)
Any outdoor protection allows normal operation of the escalator in all weathers (The landing plates shall be slip resistant when wet)
Permanent electric lighting and at least one duplex receptacle rated at 15A/120V (min) is provided in each remote machine room (Lighting to be not less than 100 lux at floor level)
Stop switch in each machinery space (except space where mainline disconnect switch is located ) is of the manually opened/closed type, has a red handle/button and is permanently marked STOP
Machine controllers are certified and labeled
Drive machine brake stops the machine automatically if the power supply is interrupted and can hold any load up to the brake rated load (stopping rate is max. of 0.91m/sec/sec)
Speed governor (where fitted) shuts down the driving machine motor at a predetermined value, not more than 40% above rated speed
Manually operate the trip mechanism to check the operation of the broken drive chain device on the drive chain
Reversal stop switch (which prevents reversal when operating in the ascending direction) is operating correctly by manually operating it
Broken step chain device shuts down power to the drive motor and brake if the step chain breaks or if excessive sag occurs in a step chain

Step up-thrust device detects a step forced upward ( in the lower transition curve) and cause the escalator to stop before the detected step reaches the combplate (maximum upward displacement is 5 mm at the lower landing)
Verify the missing step device will detect a missing step and bring the escalator to a stop before the gap emerges from the comb

Operation of the step level devices (top and bottom of the escalator) when a minimum of 3mm downward displacement occurs in a riser (Power to the machine shall cut off before the step enters the combplate)
Steps, step chains and trusses comply with manufacturer specifications
A code date plate is available for the machine (states code and edition in effect at time of installation and any alteration)
Response to a smoke detector alarm after at least 15 seconds, causes power interruption to the driving machine motor and brake
